spec_title: Defect: Container Data Persistence — Missing Volume Mounts
notes: Scope expanded same night: two compounding failures. (1) Missing volume mounts — immediate patches shipped, systematic audit still open. (2) Backup system not functional for file data — Tier 1 local script never written; S3 and Dropbox receive Postgres + agent_logs only, no curator_archive or other file data. spec #122 was marked closed but Tier 1 was never built. Build backup_local.sh before this can close.

Claude Code implements: openclaw_backend.py WS client, COS_BACKEND_TYPE env-var switch in init_backend(), Dockerfile.openclaw-cos, openclaw-cos compose service, scripts/cos_ab_test.py A/B harness. Memory judgment stays in coordination layer (not in backend). See plan for full implementation detail.

spec_title: Three-Tier Production Backup — Local EC2 + S3 + Dropbox
notes: Tier 1 (local EC2 backup_local.sh) live since 2026-07-05. Tier 2 (S3) live as of 2026-07-09: bucket minimoi-backups (versioned, AES-256, 90-day lifecycle), IAM instance role policy minimoi-s3-backup, backup_s3.sh deployed, cron 3am UTC daily, Telegram failure alert via ERR trap. First 5 backups confirmed in S3. Tier 3 (Dropbox) deferred indefinitely — backup_dropbox.sh and cron stub in place; blocked on rclone install + Dropbox OAuth browser flow when needed.

spec_title: AI Observations path fix — port intelligence job to EC2
blocked_reason: Spec required from OpenClaw before build.
notes: curator_intelligence.py writes to Path.home()/'.openclaw'/'workspace'/ — hardcoded to ~/.openclaw/workspace/ which on EC2 is /root/.openclaw/workspace/ (container-local, not mounted). curator_server.py reads from /app/data/curator/intelligence_*.json (volume-mounted). Two options: (A) run job via docker exec on EC2 + set CURATOR_DATA_DIR to redirect output, or (B) sync Mac job output to EC2 after each run. 31 historical files SCPed to /tmp/intelligence_restore/ on EC2 (Mar–Apr 2026). Anthropic API key confirmed in SSM (/minimoi/production/anthropic_api_key). Investigation complete 2026-07-05 — no code touched.

spec_title: Split Guild into standalone Build domain and CoS domain
blocked_reason: Gates on #117 Guild navigation redesign completion — do not start before #117 is done
notes: Guild currently does three jobs: Build (specs, queue, roadmap — shareable with collaborators), CoS (career, operations, agent coordination — private to Robert), and navigation container. Separate into two standalone domains. Build domain becomes shareable — appropriate for external review by collaborators or the T-Mobile AI team. CoS domain stays private, owns the memory layer and OpenClaw integration.

spec_title: Repurpose Guild CoS agent as Master Craftsman — build quality guardian
notes: Once CoS moves to its own domain (#128), the current Guild CoS agent is repurposed rather than deprecated. New role: Master Craftsman — focused on build quality, code review, domain template compliance, and standards enforcement. Fits the Guild metaphor naturally: craftsmen (builders), master craftsman (quality/standards), CoS (operations/coordination). Three distinct roles, three distinct surfaces. Rename and reframe the agent — do not delete the infrastructure.

spec_title: Automate docs/specs and build-queue sync into CI/CD deploy
blocked_reason: Idea only — no design session yet. Needs Robert + design discussion on whether/how to fold this into deploy.yml.
notes: scripts/sync_docs.sh (docs/design/*, docs/specs/*, build_queue.json -> EC2 via SSM pull from GitHub raw URLs) is a manual, Mac-run step, not part of the automated deploy.yml pipeline. Surfaced 2026-07-22 when spec #140 (Phase 2 repo reorg) merged to main but its file 404d on the Guild dashboard until sync_docs.sh was run by hand. OPERATIONS.md already documents this as expected current behavior ("A git push alone does not make a new spec visible in prod"), so this is a known gap, not a new bug. Open questions for the future design session: fold the pull into the existing deploy.yml GitHub Actions job vs. keep it a separate manual/scheduled step; whether root docs (ARCHITECTURE.md, OPERATIONS.md, ROADMAP.md) should join the synced set; failure/retry behavior if the SSM pull fails mid-deploy.
